notes: Per-test-id count budget for Bandit findings in api/core/kernel.
Initial run on 2026-05-28 locked the current state — every count above the baseline fails CI.
Goal: ratchet downward by triaging one test-id at a time per follow-up PR.
Test-id legend (https://bandit.readthedocs.io/en/latest/plugins/index.html):
B104 hardcoded_bind_all_interfaces — e.g. uvicorn host="0.0.0.0" in tests/scripts. Audit risk: low when bound to loopback in compose (audit Mo7 already enforces).
B105 hardcoded_password_string — mostly test fixtures and example strings; needs case-by-case audit.
B107 hardcoded_password_funcarg — same.
B108 hardcoded_tmp_directory — /tmp paths. Audit risk: low on Linux, prefer tempfile.
B110 try_except_pass — the most-suppressed exceptions are the M3 `_silent_log.debug` legacy pattern. Already gated by tests/test_no_new_silent_swallows.py.
B112 try_except_continue — same family.
B310 urllib_urlopen — direct urlopen calls; audit risk: medium (no SSRF defense). Migrate to httpx where possible.
B311 random_for_secrets — `random` instead of `secrets`. Audit: review each call site.
B324 hashlib_insecure — MD5/SHA1 in non-security contexts (cache keys, ETags). Usually safe but worth annotating.
B404 import_subprocess — flags every `import subprocess`. Audit risk: low (the dangerous form is shell=True which we already block manually).
B603 subprocess_without_shell — legitimate subprocess.run with shell=False. Mostly safe; the gate ensures no new shell=True sneaks in (B602).
B607 start_process_with_partial_path — subprocess calls without absolute paths. Audit risk: low in containerized runtime.
B608 hardcoded_sql_expressions — the two memory/postgres_backend.py f-string SQL sites (Mo1 already validates table_name; the warning persists because Bandit doesn't see the runtime guard).
B701 jinja2_autoescape_false — single occurrence; needs review.
To lower a budget: fix N occurrences of test-id X in a dedicated PR,
then regenerate this baseline with `bandit -r api core kernel -f json` and update the counts.
Adding a new test-id to the budget requires a written justification in a PR description.
